name: Saint Barthélemy (France)

colors.symbolism: the field of the local flag

description: A white field charged with the territorial coat of arms in the centre: a blue shield bearing three gold fleurs-de-lis, a red Maltese cross, and three crowns, supported by pelicans, with a motto scroll below.
history: The official flag of Saint Barthélemy is the French Tricolore; this local flag, featuring the territorial coat of arms on a white field, is used unofficially by the Collectivity. Saint Barthélemy was a French possession from 1648, sold to Sweden in 1784 (whence the three crowns in the arms), and resold to France in 1878. It became a separate French Overseas Collectivity (COM) on 22 February 2007, having previously been a commune of Guadeloupe.

features.symbolism: white field bearing the territorial coat of arms

features.role: Coat of arms of Saint Barthélemy
features.symbolism: The blue shield bears three gold fleurs-de-lis (recalling French royal heraldry), a Maltese cross (recalling the Knights of Malta who briefly owned the island), and three crowns (recalling the period of Swedish rule, 1784–1878). The shield is supported by two pelicans, with the motto 'Ouanalao' (the Carib name for the island) below.
